title: Administration Director
description:
Job Description

  • This is a dual role as the company’s compliance lead and senior administrator in Singapore, responsible for overseeing day-to-day administrative and compliance operations, supervising team members, and acting as a key liaison with the Japan-based Head Office.
  • Key responsibilities include reviewing and enhancing the Company’s compliance policies and procedures by interpreting Japan-based HQ standards (primarily in Japanese) and aligning them with Singapore regulatory requirements and local business needs. The role involves ongoing monitoring of the AML/CFT and Anti-Bribery & Corruption (ABC) framework, including management reporting and remediation tracking.
  • The position also requires drafting, reviewing and submitting formal governance and compliance materials in Japanese for HQ and group companies, supporting regulatory, audit and inspection matters, and preparing documentation and response materials as required.
  • In a lean office environment, the role supports broader corporate operations such as HR coordination, finance/accounting and tax administration, while ensuring proper documentation and internal controls.
  • The role requires attending and supporting meetings facilitated in Japanese, including minutes taking, issue tracking and follow-up actions, and provides day-to-day supervision and guidance to administrative and operational staff to ensure compliance with internal policies and local regulatory requirements.

Job Requirements

Must-have:

  • Minimum Bachelor’s degree.
  • At least 7 years of professional experience in the financial sector.
  • Proven experience supporting or reporting into a Japan-based HQ / Japan-led governance structure, such as implementing HQ policies locally, preparing HQ control reports, and handling HQ queries.
  • The role requires regular, direct communication in Japanese with Japanese financial institutions (such as credit cooperatives) and their corporate clients, including explaining policies, handling routine operational inquiries, and supporting coordination matters. Professional-level Japanese proficiency (written and spoken, equivalent to BJT J1+ or JLPT N1) is therefore essential to ensure accurate, real-time communication without reliance on translation or interpretation support.
  • Strong communication skills for coordination with auditors, advisers and internal stakeholders.

Other Key Skills:

  • Strong judgement, confidentiality mindset and stakeholder management skills.
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities under tight timelines.

Preferred:

  • Experience in managing recurring HQ reports and facilitating meetings conducted in Japanese.
  • Practical experience in financial institution operations, such as securities investment, lending/credit administration, or related front-to-back office processes.
  • Hands-on experience in accounting, financial closing, or operational finance processes, including handling journal entries and supporting month-end or year-end closing.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
